subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF, World Bank, V-Dem/Freedom House, RSF, HRW, Amnesty, UN Group of Experts, Crisis Group); Rwandan state media used only for self-reported program/disaster figures, cross-checked; economic data IMF-validated.
A developmental-state success on delivery (low corruption, high capacity, health, safety, growth) sitting on a hard authoritarian ceiling and active, sanctioned DRC-war involvement.

Standout split: genuine delivery strengths vs a personalist autocracy, transnational repression, the M23/DRC war drawing 2025 sanctions, and rising debt/inflation. Overall: negative on the forward DRC-war/sanctions tail.

economic.notes: Strong growth on an aid- and debt-dependent base, with a minerals flag and a managed currency.

fiscal_state.notes: Strong ~7-9% growth but debt projected ~86% GDP by 2026 (airport borrowing), donor-dependent, on a new IMF arrangement. (IMF Art IV/PCI Dec 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: A managed franc with a reactive central bank (a rate hike as inflation broke band) and limited true independence. (IMF; central bank)

inflation.notes: Re-accelerated to ~9%, above the band. (central bank)

banking_stability.notes: Sound (low NPLs, expanding credit), still developing/shallow. (central bank)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: A services/tourism/conference hub plus coffee/tea and minerals (partly DRC-sourced - a sanctions/reputation flag); a development model but aid-dependent and small. (Crisis Group; UN GoE)

institutional.notes: Genuinely high state capacity and low corruption, but no political-sphere rule of law and a repressed press.

rule_of_law.notes: A split - genuinely low corruption and high state capacity, but no judicial independence on politics. (Transparency International; Freedom House 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: IMF-engaged and usable, with some independent GDP-credibility questions. (IMF; academic critiques)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A standout developmental-state strength, with efficient delivery. (World Bank; BTI 2026)

press_freedom.notes: A poor and falling RSF standing, with surveillance, arrests, and exiled-journalist disappearances. (RSF 2025; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: Highly stable near-term under personalist rule, but with severely restricted liberties and transnational repression.

political_stability.notes: An entrenched leader re-elected with ~99%, highly stable near-term but personalist with a succession fat-tail. (Freedom House 2025)

civil_liberties.notes: Opposition disqualified, detention, and transnational repression. (Freedom House; Amnesty)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Business-friendly entry and refugee-hosting, but an authoritarian ceiling with no political belonging. (HRW)

social_cohesion.notes: Real post-genocide order and reconciliation, but enforced, with ethnic discourse tightly policed. (BTI 2026)

demographics.notes: Young and fast-growing but dense, with a land/jobs strain. (World Bank)

geopolitical.notes: A Western security partner increasingly sanctioned over deep DRC-war involvement.

alliance_reliability.notes: A Western security/peacekeeping partner, increasingly sanctioned over the DRC. (Crisis Group; Al Jazeera)

conflict_involvement.notes: Deep involvement in the DRC conflict - backing the M23 rebels who seized Goma and territory in 2025, UN-documented. (UN Group of Experts; Crisis Group)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: 2025 Western sanctions and aid cuts over the DRC; otherwise open. (Al Jazeera)

physical_practical.notes: A health-system success, good regional infrastructure, and high safety, against flood/landslide and land pressure.

climate.notes: Landslides/floods on steep terrain, but notably strong governance/readiness for the region. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: A genuine development success - a strong community-health system with high coverage for its income level. (MoH; WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Good and improving for the region - a clean, safe capital, good roads, and a new airport. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Among Africa's safest - a genuine strength. (UK FCDO)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration, with a still-nascent disaster-risk-finance strategy. (World Bank)

summary: A developmental-state standout on delivery - genuinely low corruption, high state capacity, a successful community-health system, among Africa's lowest crime, good infrastructure, and strong growth - sitting on a hard authoritarian ceiling: no political-sphere rule of law, a leader re-elected with ~99%, severely restricted liberties, and transnational repression of exiles. The decisive forward risk is geopolitical: deep, UN-documented involvement in the DRC conflict (backing the M23 rebels who seized Goma in 2025) has drawn 2025 Western sanctions and aid cuts, atop rising debt and inflation. The delivery strengths are real, but the DRC-war/sanctions tail and authoritarian ceiling tilt the outcome distribution negative.
